About

Builder by nature.
Strategist by training.

I'm a marketing strategist and AI builder finishing my MSc at Clemson University (May 2026). Before grad school, I built and scaled O'Nattys Apparel from scratch, 300+ wholesale B2B clients, two full collections, all before age 19. That experience taught me more about sales, brand, and product-market fit than any classroom could.

Most recently I spent a year as a Beer Sales Scout with Muskoka Brewery, managing 100+ retail accounts across GTA East, building shelf presence, running event activations, and learning how consumer brands actually move product at ground level.

At Clemson's Career Innovation Lab, I'm applying Claude API, Python, and n8n to build AI tools that solve real workflow problems. I care about the intersection of strategy, technology, and the human behaviours that make brands actually work.

⚡
Speed to Execution
I build fast. Real projects, real code, real results. Not decks that sit in folders.
🎯
Strategy-First
Every tactic connects to a business objective. I don't execute without knowing why.
🤖
AI-Native
I use AI as a force multiplier across research, automation, and creative workflows.
📦
Operator Mindset
From managing wholesale B2B accounts to running field activations, I get things done.

Creator Analytics Dashboard
Live analytics dashboard built for my friend Nolan's Instagram & TikTok, a men's self-improvement creator pushing toward 10K followers. Case Studies

Strategic work.

Graduate-level marketing strategy applied to real brands: behavioral economics, media planning, and growth frameworks.

Amazon DSP · Gratitude Mattresses
$12.5M
Awakening the Flywheel
"Revenue up 79.8%, but driven by price, not volume."
Proposed a $12.5M Amazon DSP media strategy targeting +20% organic rank improvement. Diagnosed a hidden unit volume decline (−14.7%) masked by a price hike driving apparent revenue growth. Restructured the funnel: $5MM Awareness / $3.8MM Consideration / $1.2MM Conversion, with custom audience segments and DSP-specific creative briefs for each stage.
View Strategy Deck
TD Bank · Behavioral Economics
40%
The Default Nudge
"40% of Canadian millennials have under $500 saved."
Designed a behavioral economics intervention using default-choice architecture: auto-transfer 5% of payroll to a locked savings account, opt-out only. 600 simulated participants, P<.005 significance, 2-year quasi-experimental design. Applied Thaler & Sunstein's nudge theory to a real financial services context to drive measurable savings behaviour change.
View Full Assignment
Atelier Design & Development · Greenville SC
2×
$250K to $500K Revenue
"A design-build firm with capacity, not clients."
Built a comprehensive GTM strategy for a Greenville, SC design-build firm targeting 2× revenue in 18 months. Identified a $400M–$1.2B SAM in the luxury residential renovation market. Modeled a 60-project annual capacity scenario with tiered service lines, referral infrastructure, and digital brand positioning to attract higher-value clients.
View Strategy Deck
Vansco Inc. · Wire Harness Manufacturing
$650M
Breaking the 95% Dependency
"95% of revenue from a single customer: Eaton."
Developed a full GTM strategy for a wire harness manufacturer with dangerous OEM concentration. Target: reduce Eaton dependency to <75% by 2028. $650M addressable market identified across industrial, EV, and defense segments. Built complete go-to-market sequencing, sales motion, and channel strategy to diversify the revenue base.
View Strategy Deck
Entrepreneurship

O'Nattys Apparel.

At 19, I built O'Nattys Apparel from nothing into a wholesale streetwear operation with 300+ B2B clients across Ontario. No co-founder. No funding. Just a product idea, a Shopify store, and the willingness to cold-call boutiques and market operators until they said yes.

I designed the hats in Photoshop, managed overseas manufacturing relationships, handled customs and logistics, built a wholesale pricing model, and ran two complete seasonal collections, the SS 2023 White Collection and SS 2024 Black Collection. The brand had a distinctive identity and a real customer base.

In 2023, I made the decision to step back and start university, rather than running the brand into the ground trying to do both. Two years of operation compressed years of real business education: sales, ops, supply chain, brand, and capital management all at once.
